content:
You are now a domain expert in requirements analysis.
You need to complete the following workflow:

Step 1: Read and understand the requirement document, and generate structured extraction results.
Step 2: Based on the extracted requirements, generate the actions that exist in the system.
Step 3: Based on the extracted requirements, generate the scenarios that exist in the system. Each scenario should be composed of actions.

First, complete Step 1: read and understand the requirement document and generate structured extraction results.
Below is a requirement document. Read and understand this document, complete the following tasks, and output the results according to the provided [Output Format].

#1 Identify the model description in the requirement document and provide a natural language introduction of the model

[Output Format]
Model Name:
Model Description:

#2 Analyze the document content and extract all requirement items

[Output Format]
Requirement Item 1:
    Requirement Description:
    Original Text:

Requirement Item 2:
    Requirement Description:
    Original Text:
...

#3 Extract the model input/output variable information(only in the table, do not construct new params), filling in the original text

[Output Format]
Variable 1:
    Variable Name:
    Type: integer / real / boolean
    Description:
    Port Type: Input / Output
    Is Constant: Yes / No

Variable 2:
    Variable Name:
    Type: integer / real / boolean
    Description:
    Port Type: Input / Output
    Is Constant: Yes / No
...

#4 Analyze the document content and extract the model’s Test Config information, directly filling in the original text

[Output Format]
Test Configuration 1:
Test Configuration 2:

Key Note:
Do not add any characters that are irrelevant to the required output format in the output.

2) Tustin Integrator
Model:  ‘integrator_12B.mdl’
Description:  A common flight control utility for computing the Tustin Integration of a signal yout = T/2*(xin + xinpv)+ ypv.  The algorithm bounds the allowable integration range with a position limiter, with TL as the Top Limit, and BL as the Bottom Limit.  Other inputs are the signal to be integrated (xin), the time step (dt), a Boolean reset flag (reset), and the initial condition upon a reset condition (ic).   A provision has been implemented for the limiter functionality in the algorithm.  If the user plumbs a TL value that is less than BL, the algorithm will swap these numerical values to correctly bound the signal.
===== TABLE 1 =====
Input Scope	Name	#	Type	Description
Global	xin	1	Double	Input Signal to be integrated with the Tustin method
Global	T	2	Double	Time step quantified by rate of execution
Global	TL	3	Double	Top Limit bounding the output, yout.
Global	BL	4	Double	Bottom Limit bounding the output, yout.
Global	reset	5	Boolean	Reset control, initializes output to ic value.
Global	ic	6	Double	Initial Condition for yout when in reset mode.
===== TABLE 2 =====
Output Scope	Name	#	Type	Description
Global	yout	1	Double	Output Signal result of Tustin integration method on xin
Definitions:
Normal operation: the integrator is not in reset mode, and the output is within the specified limits (TL and BL).
ypv:  prior yout value
xinpv:  prior xin input value
SP:  Saturation Point
Requirements:
When Reset is True and the Initial Condition (ic) is bounded by the provided Top and Bottom Limits (BL <= ic <= TL), the Output (yout) shall equal the Initial Condition (ic).
The Output (yout) shall be bounded by the provided Top and Bottom limits (TL and BL)
When in normal operation, the output shall be the result of the equation, yout = T/2*(xin + xinpv)+ ypv
Test config:
xin is parameterized as a PCHIP-interpolated smooth signal, defined by three control points c1,c2,c3, --1<=xin<=1
-10<=TL<=10
-10<=BL<=10
t = 20 (t is simulation time)
-10<=ic<=10
T = 0.1

Model Name: integrator_12B.mdl
Model Description: A common flight control utility for computing the Tustin Integration of a signal yout = T/2*(xin + xinpv)+ ypv. The algorithm bounds the allowable integration range with a position limiter, with TL as the Top Limit, and BL as the Bottom Limit. Other inputs are the signal to be integrated (xin), the time step (dt), a Boolean reset flag (reset), and the initial condition upon a reset condition (ic). A provision has been implemented for the limiter functionality in the algorithm. If the user plumbs a TL value that is less than BL, the algorithm will swap these numerical values to correctly bound the signal.

Requirement Item 1:
    Requirement Description: Reset Condition Requirement
    Original Text: When Reset is True and the Initial Condition (ic) is bounded by the provided Top and Bottom Limits (BL <= ic <= TL), the Output (yout) shall equal the Initial Condition (ic).

Requirement Item 2:
    Requirement Description: Output Limiting Requirement
    Original Text: The Output (yout) shall be bounded by the provided Top and Bottom limits (TL and BL)

Requirement Item 3:
    Requirement Description: Normal Operation Requirement
    Original Text: When in normal operation, the output shall be the result of the equation, yout = T/2*(xin + xinpv)+ ypv

Variable 1:
    Variable Name: xin
    Type: real
    Description: Input Signal to be integrated with the Tustin method
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 2:
    Variable Name: T
    Type: real
    Description: Time step quantified by rate of execution
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 3:
    Variable Name: TL
    Type: real
    Description: Top Limit bounding the output, yout.
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 4:
    Variable Name: BL
    Type: real
    Description: Bottom Limit bounding the output, yout.
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 5:
    Variable Name: reset
    Type: boolean
    Description: Reset control, initializes output to ic value.
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 6:
    Variable Name: ic
    Type: real
    Description: Initial Condition for yout when in reset mode.
    Port Type: Input
    Is Constant: No

Variable 7:
    Variable Name: yout
    Type: real
    Description: Output Signal result of Tustin integration method on xin
    Port Type: Output
    Is Constant: No

Test Configuration 1: xin is parameterized as a PCHIP-interpolated smooth signal, defined by three control points c1,c2,c3, --1<=xin<=1
Test Configuration 2: -10<=TL<=10
Test Configuration 3: -10<=BL<=10
Test Configuration 4: t = 20 (t is simulation time)
Test Configuration 5: -10<=ic<=10
Test Configuration 6: T = 0.1

**Complete Step 2:** 

Based on the extracted requirement results, generate the actions that exist in the system:
output the typical actions within the system input domain.
An action is an excitation of the system in the form of a temporal input sequence.

**Output Format**(Just output the aciton set, do not output any other characters):
Action 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description:
Input Constraint:
Temporal Constraint:

Action 2:
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description:
Input Constraint:
Temporal Constraint:
...

**Example**

Action Example 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description: Light throttle press in 3s, where “light” means throttle < 0.3
Input Constraint: throttle < 0.3
Temporal Constraint: duration <= 3

Action Example 2:
(If throttle is a parameterized variable defined by PCHIP control points, with control points c1, c2, c3)
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description: Throttle gradually increases, and no braking
Input Constraint: c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3 ∧ brake == 0
Temporal Constraint: true

**Key Notes 1: Form of Constraints**

1. The allowed symbols in constraints include `<, <=, >, >=, ∧,(),abs()`.(`∨` `==`is not not allowed, action must be atomic)
   Forms like `a < b < c` are not allowed; only forms like `a < b ∧ b < c` are permitted.
2. Input constraints must not contain natural language, and expressions such as `ia(t)` are not allowed.
   Use `ia` directly instead.
3. Input constraints should contain **only** model input variables, control-point variables from the test configuration,
   Model output variables or other constructed variables are not allowed.
4. If a model input variable is parameterized into control-point variables, the input constraint should include **only** the control-point variables, without including the original variable.
5. Temporal constraints specify constraints on the actual duration of the action, for example: `duration <= k`, do not use `duration == k`
   where `duration` refers to the action duration, and `k` should be a constant or an input variable.
   If no specific constraint exists, use `true`.
6. Temporal constraints and test configuration constraints should **not appear** in the input constraint.
7. The test configuration may assign a constant value to an input variable; in this case, that variable does not need to be considered again in the input constraint.

**Key Notes 2: Principles of Action Definition**

1. The occurrence of an action **must not have preconditions**.
   For example, an action must not require the system to already be in a certain state.
   System states should be triggered by one or more actions or sequences of actions.
2. If symmetric signals exist among input variables, action merging is not allowed.
   A complete list of actions must be explicitly enumerated.
3. The input constraints of actions must not overlap.
   All actions in the action set must be pairwise mutually exclusive.

**Key Notes 3: Format Correctness**

1. If an action generated during analysis does not satisfy the required constraint form,
   that action must **not** be included in the final action set.
   In this task, **output format correctness has the highest priority**.

Action 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is within limits
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ BL <= ic ∧ ic <= TL
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 2:
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is below bottom limit
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic < BL
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 3:
Action Name: A3
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is above top limit
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic > TL
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 4:
Action Name: A4
Semantic Description: Reset is true with TL < BL (limits will be swapped internally)
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL > TL
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 5:
Action Name: A5
Semantic Description: Reset is false (normal operation)
Input Constraint: reset = false
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 6:
Action Name: A6
Semantic Description: Rising input signal (c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3)
Input Constraint: c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 7:
Action Name: A7
Semantic Description: Falling input signal (c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3)
Input Constraint: c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 8:
Action Name: A8
Semantic Description: Peak input signal (c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3)
Input Constraint: c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 9:
Action Name: A9
Semantic Description: Valley input signal (c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3)
Input Constraint: c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Apply the concept of combinatorial equivalence classes, 
compute the Cartesian product of the atomic condition in the input and temporal constraints to discover additional input patterns
and generate an refined action set

Action 1:
Action Name: A1
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is within limits, rising input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ BL <= ic ∧ ic <= TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 2:
Action Name: A2
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is within limits, falling input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ BL <= ic ∧ ic <= TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 3:
Action Name: A3
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is within limits, peak input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ BL <= ic ∧ ic <= TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 4:
Action Name: A4
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is within limits, valley input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ BL <= ic ∧ ic <= TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 5:
Action Name: A5
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is below bottom limit, rising input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic < BL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 6:
Action Name: A6
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is below bottom limit, falling input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic < BL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 7:
Action Name: A7
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is below bottom limit, peak input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic < BL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 8:
Action Name: A8
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is below bottom limit, valley input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic < BL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 9:
Action Name: A9
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is above top limit, rising input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic > TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 10:
Action Name: A10
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is above top limit, falling input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic > TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 11:
Action Name: A11
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is above top limit, peak input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic > TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 12:
Action Name: A12
Semantic Description: Reset is true, TL >= BL, and initial condition is above top limit, valley input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL <= TL ∧ ic > TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 13:
Action Name: A13
Semantic Description: Reset is true with TL < BL (limits will be swapped internally), rising input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL > TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 14:
Action Name: A14
Semantic Description: Reset is true with TL < BL (limits will be swapped internally), falling input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL > TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 15:
Action Name: A15
Semantic Description: Reset is true with TL < BL (limits will be swapped internally), peak input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL > TL ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 16:
Action Name: A16
Semantic Description: Reset is true with TL < BL (limits will be swapped internally), valley input signal
Input Constraint: reset = true ∧ BL > TL ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 17:
Action Name: A17
Semantic Description: Reset is false (normal operation), rising input signal
Input Constraint: reset = false ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 18:
Action Name: A18
Semantic Description: Reset is false (normal operation), falling input signal
Input Constraint: reset = false ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 19:
Action Name: A19
Semantic Description: Reset is false (normal operation), peak input signal
Input Constraint: reset = false ∧ c1 < c2 ∧ c2 > c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Action 20:
Action Name: A20
Semantic Description: Reset is false (normal operation), valley input signal
Input Constraint: reset = false ∧ c1 > c2 ∧ c2 < c3
Temporal Constraint: true

Complete Step 3: Based on the extracted requirement results, generate the scenarios that exist in the system. Each scenario should be composed of actions.
Based on the extracted requirement items and the action set above, continue to complete the following tasks:
According to the specified format, and based on the given test configuration, randomly generate 10 scenarios that 
together cover all the actions and requirement items.

**Scenario Definition**
Let $\mathbb{A}$ denote a finite action set.
A \emph{scenario} is defined as a tuple
\[
\mathcal{S} = (\mathbf{A}, \sigma_S, \rho),
\]
where:
\begin{itemize}
  \item $\mathbf{A} = \langle \mathcal{A}_1, \mathcal{A}_2, \dots, \mathcal{A}_K \rangle$
  is an ordered sequence of actions, with $\mathcal{A}_k \in \mathbb{A}$;
  \item $\sigma_S$ denotes the semantic description of the scenario;
  \item $\rho$ denotes the identifier of the associated requirement(s).
\end{itemize}


### Output Format((Do not output any other characters)):
#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: (natural language description)
* Executable Action Set: (action names, separated by commas, e.g., A1,A2)
* Scenario Action Sequence: (ordered list enclosed in `[ ]`, where each element is an action name, e.g., [A1,A2])
* Associated Requirement:

#S2:
...

### Scenario Example:
#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: First lightly press the throttle (assumed to correspond to action A1), then lightly press the brake (assumed to correspond to action A2), and then press the throttle aggressively (assumed to correspond to action A3)
* Executable Actions: A1, A2, A3
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A1, A2, A3]
* Associated Requirement: (fill in the relevant requirement IDs)


### Key Notes 1:
The scenario semantic description should express a **feasible and random combination of action sequences**.
The scenario action sequence should be **consistent with the scenario semantic description**.

#S1:
* Scenario Semantic Description: First, the system is reset with initial condition within limits and rising input signal (A1). Then, it transitions to normal operation with falling input signal (A18).
* Executable Action Set: A1, A18
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A1, A18]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R3

#S2:
* Scenario Semantic Description: The system starts with reset condition where initial condition is below bottom limit and peak input signal (A7). Then, it switches to reset with TL<BL and valley input signal (A16).
* Executable Action Set: A7, A16
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A7, A16]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2

#S3:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Normal operation begins with valley input signal (A20). After some time, the system is reset with initial condition above top limit and falling input signal (A10).
* Executable Action Set: A20, A10
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A20, A10]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R3

#S4:
* Scenario Semantic Description: The system starts in reset mode with TL<BL and rising input signal (A13). It then enters normal operation with peak input signal (A19).
* Executable Action Set: A13, A19
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A13, A19]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2, R3

#S5:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Reset condition with initial condition within limits and falling input signal (A2) is followed by reset with initial condition above top limit and valley input signal (A12).
* Executable Action Set: A2, A12
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A2, A12]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2

#S6:
* Scenario Semantic Description: The system operates normally with rising input signal (A17). After a period, it enters reset mode with TL<BL and peak input signal (A15).
* Executable Action Set: A17, A15
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A17, A15]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R3

#S7:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Reset with initial condition below bottom limit and valley input signal (A8) is followed by reset with initial condition above top limit and rising input signal (A9).
* Executable Action Set: A8, A9
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A8, A9]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2

#S8:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Normal operation with falling input signal (A18) transitions to normal operation with peak input signal (A19).
* Executable Action Set: A18, A19
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A18, A19]
* Associated Requirement: R2, R3

#S9:
* Scenario Semantic Description: Reset with TL<BL and falling input signal (A14) is followed by reset with initial condition within limits and peak input signal (A3).
* Executable Action Set: A14, A3
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A14, A3]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2

#S10:
* Scenario Semantic Description: The system starts in reset mode with initial condition above top limit and peak input signal (A11). It then transitions to reset with initial condition below bottom limit and falling input signal (A6).
* Executable Action Set: A11, A6
* Scenario Action Sequence: [A11, A6]
* Associated Requirement: R1, R2
